key_insight: A hierarchical NMPC scheme with neural thermal models optimizes BEV HVAC and battery thermal control to reduce energy use while maintaining cabin comfort and battery safety.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical control framework for extending EV range by coordinating cabin and battery thermal management under realistic driving conditions.
method: Supervisory NMPC balances energy cost vs. cabin temperature tracking over a long horizon.
Lower-layer NMPC applies local corrections to handle disturbances and model mismatch.
Neural network prediction models capture the nonlinear coupled thermal dynamics of HVAC and BTM.
evidence_signal: The method was validated in a high-fidelity BEV thermal simulator and benchmarked against an industrial rule-based controller over the WLTC driving cycle, showing lower energy consumption while meeting constraints.
watch_item: Performance depends on simulator fidelity and the neural models’ ability to generalize to unseen thermal conditions and vehicle usage patterns.
paper_title: Thermal management optimization of Battery Electric Vehicles via hierarchical NMPC with CMO-trained Neural Models
